title: Enumerations

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific C: Beginner to Intermediate draft for Enumerations; human standards, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: GNU C Language Introduction and Reference Manual; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Enumerations
sources.claim: An enumeration declares named integer constants and an enumerated type, improving domain vocabulary while remaining subject to implementation integer representation rules.

canonicalExplanation.summary: An enumeration declares named integer constants and an enumerated type, improving domain vocabulary while remaining subject to implementation integer representation rules.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: enum Status names STATUS_PENDING, STATUS_READY, and STATUS_FAILED so a switch can communicate domain cases clearly. Your goal is to use an enum with exhaustive control flow and a safe invalid-value policy. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: One-dimensional arrays; Addresses and dereference; if and switch. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language rule and operational model
sections.body: An enumeration declares named integer constants and an enumerated type, improving domain vocabulary while remaining subject to implementation integer representation rules. This rule is applied to concrete C source and observable behavior, not treated as a vocabulary definition alone.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: C enums do not automatically reject every out-of-domain integer conversion, underlying representation can vary, and duplicate enumerator values are possible. Therefore the draft explicitly rejects this beginner failure model: A C enum is a runtime-checked closed set that can never hold a value outside its listed enumerators.

includes: Represent a closed set of named integral states and validate external values.
Use an enum with exhaustive control flow and a safe invalid-value policy.
excludes: C enums do not automatically reject every out-of-domain integer conversion, underlying representation can vary, and duplicate enumerator values are possible.

outcomes: Use an enum with exhaustive control flow and a safe invalid-value policy.

examples.title: Enumerations: defined case
examples.situation: enum Status names STATUS_PENDING, STATUS_READY, and STATUS_FAILED so a switch can communicate domain cases clearly.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the concrete source or state: enum Status names STATUS_PENDING, STATUS_READY, and STATUS_FAILED so a switch can communicate domain cases clearly.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the conclusion: Use enums for named integral states but validate external values explicitly; the type alone is not a universal runtime membership check.
examples.result: The defined result follows because Use enums for named integral states but validate external values explicitly; the type alone is not a universal runtime membership check.

examples.title: Enumerations: boundary case
examples.situation: Input is validated before converting an arbitrary integer to an enum-backed state because not every integer names a declared enumerator.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed source or state: Input is validated before converting an arbitrary integer to an enum-backed state because not every integer names a declared enumerator.

Step 2 — Keep this standard boundary visible: C enums do not automatically reject every out-of-domain integer conversion, underlying representation can vary, and duplicate enumerator values are possible.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting rule: A C enum is a runtime-checked closed set that can never hold a value outside its listed enumerators.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Use an enum with exhaustive control flow and a safe invalid-value policy.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: A C enum is a runtime-checked closed set that can never hold a value outside its listed enumerators.
misconceptions.correction: Use enums for named integral states but validate external values explicitly; the type alone is not a universal runtime membership check.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using concrete C rules, explain why this belief fails: A C enum is a runtime-checked closed set that can never hold a value outside its listed enumerators.

analogy.example: A manually managed workshop can model Enumerations: every tool, address, buffer, and acquired resource has an explicit valid range and owner.
usefulFor: Making low-level state, bounds, representation, ownership, and cleanup responsibilities concrete
limitations: C abstract-machine rules, undefined behavior, pointer provenance, translation, and implementation variation are stricter than a physical workshop analogy.
C enums do not automatically reject every out-of-domain integer conversion, underlying representation can vary, and duplicate enumerator values are possible.

recap.summary: You should now be able to use an enum with exhaustive control flow and a safe invalid-value policy. Keep this boundary in view: C enums do not automatically reject every out-of-domain integer conversion, underlying representation can vary, and duplicate enumerator values are possible.
